title: Fix Broken Crons: Slack accountId Issue (3 jobs)
description:
THREE cron jobs fail with 'Slack bot token missing for account default' because sub-agents ignore the accountId=tony instruction in the prompt. The jobs: Creator Habits Mid-Day Check (d6e648ea), Brain Health Report (aaf72c11), Thumbnail Pipeline Daily (165b1afa).

ROOT CAUSE: When isolated sub-agents use the message tool, they default to accountId='default' which doesn't exist. The prompt says to use accountId=tony but sub-agents don't always follow prompt instructions for tool parameters.

FIX APPROACH: Change these jobs to use delivery.mode='announce' instead of having the sub-agent call the message tool directly. This way OpenClaw handles Slack delivery automatically — the sub-agent just outputs text and OpenClaw announces it. Remove all 'message tool' instructions from the payload and set delivery.mode='announce', delivery.channel='slack', delivery.to='C0AF6B4FVMZ'.

For each job:
1. Use cron tool action=update, jobId=<id>, patch delivery to {mode:'announce', channel:'slack', to:'C0AF6B4FVMZ'}
2. Remove all 'message tool' instructions from the payload message — replace with 'Just output your findings. They will be announced to Slack automatically. Do NOT use the message tool.'
3. Re-enable the job (enabled: true)
4. Force-run it once to verify no error
5. Check cron tool action=runs to confirm lastStatus='ok'

TEST: After fix, force-run each job. Success = lastStatus is 'ok' and message appears in #cron-logs. Failure = lastStatus is 'error' with the same Slack token error.

JOB IDS:
- d6e648ea-947a-4649-a0a0-04352b314f99 (Creator Habits Mid-Day Check)
- aaf72c11-b44f-4b24-b1a7-acda0406551f (Brain Health Report)
- 165b1afa-eed5-4f85-8b0b-71b6464ee964 (Thumbnail Pipeline Daily)

title: Fix Broken Cron: Self-Healer Ollama 404
description:
The Cron Self-Healer (e42eee8e) runs on ollama/qwen3:8b and got a 404 last run — Ollama was likely down or the model wasn't loaded.

ROOT CAUSE: Ollama is not always running or qwen3:8b may not be pulled. This is a reliability issue — the self-healer itself can't self-heal.

FIX: Switch the self-healer to anthropic/claude-haiku-4-5 per TOOLS.md (Haiku for time-sensitive/reliable jobs). The self-healer is an ops-critical job — it should never fail due to Ollama being down.

STEPS:
1. Use cron tool action=update, jobId='e42eee8e-4258-460e-9580-ccff292d5f86'
2. Patch: payload.model = 'anthropic/claude-haiku-4-5'
3. Re-enable (enabled: true)
4. Force-run to verify

ALSO: The self-healer prompt tells sub-agents to fix accountId issues by editing prompts — but that doesn't actually work (sub-agents still ignore it). Update the self-healer prompt to instead recommend switching to delivery.mode='announce' as the fix pattern.

TEST: Force-run after update. Success = runs without error, produces a health report or NO_REPLY.

title: Consolidate X/Twitter Pipeline: 9 Jobs → 3
description:
Currently 9 cron jobs for X/Twitter. This is excessive and wastes resources. Consolidate to 3 jobs max.

CURRENT JOBS (9):
- X Creator Recon 4x daily (4ee024fe) — bird CLI, 4 runs/day, qwen3:30b
- X Creator Monitor 6AM (fac31572) — PowerShell monitor.ps1, qwen3:30b
- X Creator Monitor 12PM (19fbb289) — same
- X Creator Monitor 6PM (66e5dc7a) — same
- X Creator Monitor Midnight (508fbd03) — same
- X Morning Digest (d949fafb) — Sonnet, picks QT opportunities
- X Daily Digest (8b4f10f5) — Sonnet, picks QT opportunities
- X Midday Comments (9315426c) — Sonnet, drafts replies, 300s timeout
- X Afternoon Comments (95cf9fb6) — Sonnet, drafts replies, 300s timeout (took 5 min last run!)

TARGET STATE (3 jobs):
1. X Creator Monitor (2x daily, 8am + 6pm) — Combines the monitor.ps1 script AND bird CLI recon into one job. Runs on qwen3:30b. Collects data silently.
2. X Morning Digest + Comments (10am) — One Sonnet job that picks QT opportunities AND comment opportunities from overnight/morning data. Delivers to Slack.
3. X Evening Digest + Comments (5pm) — Same but for afternoon data.

STEPS:
1. Delete the 4 individual monitor jobs (fac31572, 19fbb289, 66e5dc7a, 508fbd03)
2. Update the Recon job (4ee024fe) to run 2x daily (8am, 6pm) and also run monitor.ps1
3. Delete the separate morning digest (d949fafb) — merge into a new combined digest+comments job at 10am
4. Delete the separate daily digest (8b4f10f5) — merge into a new combined digest+comments job at 5pm
5. Delete the midday comments job (9315426c)
6. Delete the afternoon comments job (95cf9fb6)
7. Create 2 new combined digest+comment jobs on Haiku (not Sonnet — these don't need Sonnet)

IMPORTANT: Before deleting anything, save current job configs to a backup file at C:\Users\chris\.openclaw\workspace-tony\backups\x-crons-backup-2026-02-18.json

TEST: After consolidation, force-run all 3 new jobs. Verify they produce output. Check that X knowledge bank files still get updated.

title: Fix Model Waste: Brain API Health Monitor (Sonnet → Haiku)
description:
Brain API Health Monitor (010c6239) runs every 15 minutes on Sonnet. That's 96 Sonnet calls/day to check if an API endpoint returns 200. Massive waste.

FIX:
1. Switch model to anthropic/claude-haiku-4-5
2. Reduce frequency to every 30 minutes (still plenty for monitoring)
3. Update schedule from '*/15 * * * *' to '*/30 * * * *'
4. Re-enable

STEPS:
1. cron action=update, jobId='010c6239-47e2-46db-91ad-2f1fa9b9147a'
2. patch: { payload: { model: 'anthropic/claude-haiku-4-5' }, schedule: { kind: 'cron', expr: '*/30 * * * *', tz: 'America/New_York' }, enabled: true }

TEST: Force-run. Verify it hits the health endpoint and stays silent on success.

title: Fix Model Waste: Ninja Body Evening (no model = Opus default)
description:
Ninja Body Evening Check-in (201500bb) has NO model specified in its payload. This means it defaults to whatever the agent's default model is — likely Opus, the most expensive option. For a wellness check-in.

FIX:
1. Set model to anthropic/claude-haiku-4-5 — this is a simple conversational check-in, Haiku is more than enough
2. BUT FIRST — this job overlaps with SuperBetter Evening Check-in (see cron-006). They may be merged. Check cron-006 before acting.

STEPS:
1. cron action=update, jobId='201500bb-9542-44df-8264-4a21ea9bf535'
2. patch: { payload: { model: 'anthropic/claude-haiku-4-5' } }

TEST: Force-run. Verify it sends a Ninja Body check-in message.

title: Merge Duplicate 9pm Check-ins: SuperBetter + Ninja Body → 1 Job
description:
Two jobs fire at 9pm EST asking Chris nearly identical questions:
- SuperBetter Evening Check-in (182e42c3) — Sonnet — asks: power-ups, bad guys, quests, resilience score
- Ninja Body Evening Check-in (201500bb) — NO MODEL (Opus?!) — asks: quest completion, power-ups, bad guys, how you feel

They're both from the SuperBetter framework. Chris gets pinged twice at 9pm with overlapping questions.

FIX: Merge into ONE evening check-in job that covers both SuperBetter core + Ninja Body specifics.

STEPS:
1. Delete Ninja Body Evening (201500bb)
2. Update SuperBetter Evening (182e42c3) to include Ninja Body quest tracking
3. Switch model from Sonnet to Haiku (it's a conversational check-in)
4. Update the prompt to reference BOTH superbetter-game.md AND superbetter-ninja-body.md
5. Set delivery.mode='announce' to avoid the accountId issue
6. Re-enable

TEST: Force-run at a test time. Verify one combined check-in message appears in Slack covering both SuperBetter + Ninja Body.

title: Merge Promise Enforcer + Reminder Checker → 1 Job
description:
Two jobs run every 30 minutes checking two separate files:
- Promise Enforcer (808a5f06) — checks PROMISES.md for due items
- Reminder Checker (9b15fcc9) — checks REMINDERS.md for due items

Both use qwen3:8b, both send to the same Slack channel, both have identical logic (check file → find due items → send → update file).

FIX: Merge into one 'Due Items Checker' that checks BOTH files in a single run.

STEPS:
1. Delete Reminder Checker (9b15fcc9)
2. Update Promise Enforcer (808a5f06) name to 'Due Items Checker'
3. Update prompt to check BOTH PROMISES.md AND REMINDERS.md
4. Switch model to anthropic/claude-haiku-4-5 (per TOOLS.md — time-sensitive jobs should use Haiku, not Ollama)
5. Keep schedule at every 30 min
6. Set delivery.mode='announce' to avoid accountId issues
7. Re-enable

TEST: Add a test reminder to REMINDERS.md with a past due date. Force-run. Verify it gets picked up and announced.

title: Fix Model Assignments: Downgrade Expensive Jobs
description:
Several jobs use more expensive models than needed. Review and downgrade:

JOBS TO DOWNGRADE:
1. Morning Brief Pipeline (1bcb51fd) — Currently Sonnet. This one is complex (multi-step pipeline) so Sonnet may be justified. KEEP as-is but evaluate if Haiku could handle it.
2. Weekly Goal Scorecard (fc35c874) — Sonnet. Complex analysis. KEEP Sonnet but add it back as enabled.
3. Ninja Body Daily Quest (1986a089) — Sonnet. Simple: read tracker, find next quest, send message. DOWNGRADE to Haiku.
4. X Digest jobs (if not deleted in cron-003) — Sonnet. Drafting tweets doesn't need Sonnet. DOWNGRADE to Haiku.

STEPS:
1. cron update Ninja Body Daily Quest → model: anthropic/claude-haiku-4-5
2. Review each remaining Sonnet job and justify or downgrade
3. Document which jobs stay on Sonnet and why

TEST: Force-run downgraded jobs. Verify output quality is acceptable on Haiku.

title: Route Crons to Proper Agents (Agent Specialization)
description:
Almost every cron targets agentId='main'. The specialized agents (youtube, engineering, family, etc.) exist but aren't being used. This means Tony's main session gets cluttered with everything.

CURRENT STATE: 30 of 32 jobs target 'main'. Only 2 exceptions: Brain API (engineering), Florida Prepaid (family).

TARGET STATE:
- X/Twitter crons → Create a dedicated X agent or route to an existing one
- Wellness crons (SuperBetter, Ninja Body, Creator Habits) → Route to a wellness/lifecoach agent
- YouTube/CJS crons → Route to youtube agent
- Infrastructure crons (backups, health monitor, self-healer) → Route to engineering agent
- Financial crons → Route to a financial agent
- Morning Brief → Keep on main (Tony) since it's cross-cutting
- Promise/Reminder checker → Keep on main (Tony)

BLOCKER: Need to verify which agents exist and have proper SOUL.md/AGENTS.md configs. Check sessions_list and each agent's workspace.

STEPS:
1. List all agents: agents_list tool
2. For each agent, verify it has a workspace with SOUL.md
3. Update cron jobs with proper agentId routing
4. For agents that don't exist yet, create tasks to set them up

NOTE: This is a bigger effort. Do NOT rush this — incorrect routing will break things. Plan first, execute second.

TEST: After routing, force-run one job per agent. Verify the job runs in the correct agent's session and output goes to the right Slack channel.

title: Re-enable All Healthy Crons After Fixes
description:
The audit disabled ALL 32 cron jobs. After fixes are applied, we need to selectively re-enable the ones that should be running.

DO NOT RE-ENABLE:
- One-shot jobs that have already fired or are obsolete
- Jobs that were merged/deleted in other tasks
- Sunday Finance Review (cf4eac6f) — was already disabled before audit
- Thumbnail pipeline progress checker (b744eca2) — one-off monitoring job

RE-ENABLE (after their respective fix tasks are done):
- All fixed/merged cron jobs from tasks cron-001 through cron-008
- Morning Brief Pipeline
- Backup jobs (daily full + quick)
- Token Usage Report
- CJS accountability jobs (Monday, Wednesday, Monthly)
- Weekly Goal Scorecard

STEPS:
1. Go through each job ID
2. Verify its fix task is marked 'done'
3. Enable it: cron action=update, jobId=X, patch={enabled:true}
4. Document final state in a summary

TEST: After re-enabling, wait for the next scheduled run of each job. Check cron runs to verify all are healthy.

title: Switch All delivery.mode='none' Jobs to 'announce' Pattern
description:
Many jobs use delivery.mode='none' and tell the sub-agent to call the message tool directly. This is the ROOT CAUSE of the accountId failures. The reliable pattern is delivery.mode='announce' where OpenClaw handles Slack delivery.

AFFECTED JOBS (delivery.mode='none' with message tool instructions in prompt):
- Promise Enforcer (808a5f06)
- Reminder Checker (9b15fcc9)
- X Morning Digest (d949fafb)
- X Daily Digest (8b4f10f5)
- X Midday Comments (9315426c)
- X Afternoon Comments (95cf9fb6)
- SuperBetter Evening (182e42c3)
- Ninja Body Evening (201500bb)
- Ninja Body Daily Quest (1986a089)
- Token Usage Report (2b4caf6e)

For each: update delivery to {mode:'announce', channel:'slack', to:'C0AF6B4FVMZ'} and remove message tool instructions from the prompt. Replace with 'Just output your findings/message. It will be announced to Slack automatically. Do NOT use the message tool.'

EXCEPTIONS: Some jobs legitimately need to stay silent (backups, monitors). Those should keep delivery.mode='none' and NOT use the message tool at all.

TEST: Force-run 2-3 converted jobs. Verify messages appear in the correct Slack channel via the announce mechanism.

title: Document Final Cron Architecture
description:
After all fixes/merges/consolidation, document the final cron architecture so we never end up with 32 unmanaged jobs again.

DELIVERABLE: C:\Users\chris\.openclaw\workspace-tony\CRON-ARCHITECTURE.md

CONTENTS:
1. Complete list of all active cron jobs with: name, schedule, model, agent, purpose, delivery method
2. Guidelines for adding new crons: model selection (when to use Haiku vs Sonnet vs Ollama), delivery pattern (always use announce), agent routing rules
3. Cost estimate: approximate daily/monthly cost of all crons
4. Monitoring: how the self-healer works, what it can/can't fix
5. Known patterns that break: accountId issue, Ollama reliability, timeout issues

TEST: N/A — this is documentation.
